perturbation.original_text: A) The initial term of this Agreement shall be three (3) calendar years from the Agreement date. After the initial term, unless terminated, this Agreement will automatically renew for periods of one (1) calendar year each.
perturbation.changed_text: A) The initial term of this Agreement shall be three (3) calendar years from the Agreement date. After the initial term, this Agreement will only renew upon written agreement by both parties, not automatically.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 2A states that the agreement will automatically renew for one-year periods after the initial term, unless terminated. The modified text changes this, stating that renewal is contingent upon written agreement by both parties. This creates an in-text contradiction about the renewal process. It introduces uncertainty about whether the agreement automatically renews or requires explicit action from both parties. If not taken seriously, can cause legal disputes about the agreement’s continued validity. It contradicts with termination terms if there is no mutual agreement, due to the change in the agreement renewal clause
perturbation.contradicted_text: A) Either party may terminate this agreement by giving written Notice to the other party if: i) The other party fails to perform or satisfy any of the conditions, covenants or obligations of this Agreement. ii) The other party files or has filed against it, a petition seeking relief under any bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganisation, moratorium, liquidation or similar law affecting creditors' rights B) In addition, Lucid may terminate this agreement by giving the Distributor Written Notice if there is any change of control, ownership or management of the Distributor. C) Either party may terminate this agreement by providing Ninety days Written Notice.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated automatic renewal after the initial term, while the changed text requires written agreement for renewal. This directly contradicts the termination terms in the 'Contradicted Text' section. If the agreement doesn't automatically renew and there's no mutual agreement to renew in writing, it affects the conditions under which the agreement can be terminated, creating a strong in-text contradiction. Specifically, it affects how A) can be enforced due to the change in the agreement renewal clause.

perturbation.original_text: 13. Prices, Terms & Conditions of Sale A) The Distributor may purchase Products from Lucid at the price published by Lucid, from time to time, in the International Distributor price list (attached). Lucid agrees to allow the Distributor a discount of thirty (30) per cent against the published International Distributor price list.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. Prices, Terms & Conditions of Sale A) The Distributor may purchase Products from Lucid at the price published by Lucid, from time to time, in the International Distributor price list (attached). Lucid agrees to allow the Distributor a discount of thirty (30) per cent against the published International Distributor price list. However, Lucid reserves the right to alter the discount percentage at any time, with or without prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that the distributor receives a 30% discount. The added phrase introduces a contradiction, allowing Lucid to change the discount at any time without notice. This creates uncertainty about the actual discount the distributor will receive and undermines the original agreement. This contradicts with the entire agreement, specifically with the Price list attached to the agreement. If the Price list can be changed and the discount can be altered at any time with prior notice, which parts of the agreement the parties agree to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 22 ENTIRE AGREEMENT: A) This Agreement, including the Price List attached hereto, as amended from time to time, constitutes the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes and replaces all previous proposals, both oral and written, negotiations, representations, commitments, writings, contracts, agreements and all other communications between the two parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause allowing Lucid to alter the discount percentage at any time, with or without prior notice. This directly contradicts Section 22, which states that the agreement, including the price list, constitutes the entire understanding between the parties. If the discount can be changed unilaterally, the 'ENTIRE AGREEMENT' clause is undermined as the agreed-upon terms are no longer fixed and certain. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it introduces an element of uncertainty and potential unilateral modification that conflicts with the agreement's stated completeness and finality.

perturbation.original_text: B) The Distributor agrees to act as a Distributor for Lucid within the above Territories and not to solicit any sales of the Product(s) outside the above Territories.
perturbation.changed_text: B) The Distributor agrees to act as a Distributor for Lucid within the above Territories and not to solicit any sales of the Product(s) outside the above Territories. However, in situations of unforeseen market demand, the Distributor may, with *verbal* approval from Lucid, engage in limited sales activities outside the designated territory.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 4B strictly prohibits the distributor from soliciting sales outside the designated territory. Adding 'However, in situations of unforeseen market demand, the Distributor may, with *verbal* approval from Lucid, engage in limited sales activities outside the designated territory' introduces a contradiction. It allows for exceptions based on 'unforeseen market demand' with just verbal approval, undermining the original restriction. There's no record of approval and no limitations on 'limited sales activities'. This contradicts the clause that has been added to the text, by stating it can solicit sales to outside the territory with *verbal* approval, the *verbal* part adds the in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A) The Distributor is appointed as an exclusive Distributor within the following territories: [*]. B) The Distributor agrees to act as a Distributor for Lucid within the above Territories and not to solicit any sales of the Product(s) outside the above Territories. However, in situations of unforeseen market demand, the Distributor may, with verbal approval from Lucid, engage in limited sales activities outside the designated territory.
perturbation.justification: The addition of the sentence 'However, in situations of unforeseen market demand, the Distributor may, with verbal approval from Lucid, engage in limited sales activities outside the designated territory' directly contradicts the previous statement: 'The Distributor agrees to act as a Distributor for Lucid within the above Territories and not to solicit any sales of the Product(s) outside the above Territories.' The original text establishes a strict prohibition against soliciting sales outside the designated territories. The changed text introduces an exception based on 'unforeseen market demand' with only 'verbal approval,' effectively undermining the original restriction and creating a contradiction. This creates a conflict and uncertainty about the obligations of the Distributor, fulfilling the criteria for in-text contradiction as laid out in the instructions.
